task.title: Reject hidden invalid stream versions before append
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/406 by rejecting a stored stream version below one before payload encoding even when a higher valid row would otherwise hide it from the current-version lookup.
acceptance_criteria: Append detects an invalid stored stream version when invalid and valid rows coexist in one stream.
The failure is EventLogError::InvalidStoredVersion and occurs before caller serialization.
The rejected append writes no event.
The valid maximum version remains the current stream position when all stored versions are valid.
The lookup remains indexed, the behavior is documented, and pinned local and pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added a focused regression with stored stream versions zero and one plus a serializer that must not run.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test panicked with 'a rejected append must not encode its event', proving the MAX-only lookup masked version zero and allowed payload encoding.

attempts.summary: Validated the minimum stored version before returning the maximum as the current stream position.
attempts.patch: The focused regression returned InvalidStoredVersion(0), did not invoke serialization, and confirmed the stored row count remained two.

attempts.summary: Refactored the minimum and maximum lookup after an independent pre-commit review identified a potential aggregate scan on every append.
attempts.diagnostic: SQLite EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN showed each scalar subquery searching the events primary key by stream_id instead of one combined aggregate over the stream.
attempts.patch: Used separate MIN and MAX scalar subqueries in one statement so corruption detection and current-position lookup retain indexed endpoint searches.

attempts.summary: Verified and merged the bounded fix.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused regression, just verify, and cargo doc passed; Rust quality gate run 29332734324 passed; the required CodeRabbit status passed, although its walkthrough comment reported a GitHub timeout while posting review comments; and pull request 407 squash-merged as fe211f9.
attempts.patch: No further correctness, test, or documentation correction was required.

outcome.summary: Append now detects any stored stream version below one before caller serialization even when a higher valid version exists, while indexed maximum lookup still supplies the current position.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test -p vela-kernel --test event_log rejects_a_hidden_invalid_stored_version_before_encoding -- --exact
SQLite EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN for minimum and maximum scalar subqueries: both search the events primary key by stream_id
Independent pre-commit diff review for issue 406: aggregate-scan concern fixed; no blocking findings remained
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29332734324 for pull request 407
Required CodeRabbit status for pull request 407; status passed, walkthrough comment disclosed a GitHub timeout while posting review comments

lessons: A maximum-only query can hide lower-bound corruption when invalid and valid rows coexist in one stream.
Corruption regressions should combine the invalid boundary with a valid higher row so they exercise masking rather than only direct conversion.
Rejecting impossible persistent state before caller serialization preserves deterministic error precedence and avoids unnecessary or unsafe caller code.
Correctness checks on a hot append path should preserve indexed endpoint lookup; query plans are useful review evidence when an aggregate shape changes.
Required review status and review-comment delivery are distinct signals and should be recorded separately when an integration reports a posting timeout.
